title: lichen_pubkey_to_iid() hardcodes 32-byte pubkey length without compile-time assertion
description:
In ipv6_addr.c:64, tc_sha256_update() is called with hardcoded length 32:
tc_sha256_update(&sha_state, pubkey, 32);

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.c:64

The header declares pubkey as 'const uint8_t *' without size information. If a caller passes a shorter buffer, this causes an out-of-bounds read. The function relies entirely on caller discipline.

Impact: If a future caller passes less than 32 bytes, out-of-bounds read occurs. Ed25519 public keys are 32 bytes, so this is correct for intended usage, but the interface provides no protection.

Recommendation: 
1. Add a compile-time static_assert or BUILD_ASSERT for Ed25519 key size
2. Document the 32-byte requirement prominently in the header
3. Consider passing length parameter for defensive coding

Lower priority as this is an API design issue rather than a bug in current code.
